publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 65-66. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. This will go by James very early tomorrow; as Mr Halliday says there is some excellent Harrier Hunting near (at least a few miles off) on the Moors tomorrow; & wishes me in case it be fine enough, to have Tag-Rag over. So I have desired

the Coachman to bring him to me the first thing. James will return here too. I hope you will not be displeased; but as the saddle horses are doing absolutely nothing, (& John can drive you if you use the carriage), I thought you would not object. And I wish to have the Coachman to take care of Taggy; who is too valuable to trust to

You must not detain them in coming back; as we may have to set out very early. You send the Post-Boy on, do you not? Tho' he should have orders if I am out beyond a certain hour, not to wait for me, & so miss your post at Porlock.--
I have little hope of the weather; but the Hallidays have more. I however ought to have Tag-Rag for the wet moors. He is
so strong, & so clever; & will not get me into a scrape.
Captain Prevost is pleasing & handsome; & very much obliged to you for your kindess about his brother.
I must get them to come over to Ashley. Captain M-- is expected tomorrow.
Mrs Prevost is a very nice girl. They have only been married a week.
I was in very good time today; & your M'Intosh very useful.-- I hope you won't be angry; but I should be
vexed to lose the use which the horses might be of possibly, this once while I am in the West this year.
